hypothesis: If combining sequential knowledge transfer strategies (pre-training on large chemical databases, fine-tuning on domain-specific CTF structures, and reinforcement learning with expert-defined physicochemical reward functions) is applied to generative molecular models, then the resulting configurations will produce novel, valid CTF candidate molecules that satisfy expert-defined structural criteria for supercapacitor applications at higher rates than models using any single knowledge transfer strategy alone, because layered knowledge transfer progressively constrains the chemical space toward domain-relevant structures while maintaining sufficient diversity for de novo discovery.
